text.title: Loop Selection, Instrumentation, and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: How to pick the right loop type, instrument share events, and avoid common agent mistakes.

list.item: Productivity tool → WOM (primary) + Inherent (secondary if team features exist)

rule.statement: Run at most 2 loops in parallel; instrument them with distinct loop_type property on all invite events so K can be computed per loop separately.
rule.rationale: Attribution between loops is messy without explicit tagging. Running 4 loops at once makes it impossible to know which is contributing and which is wasting engineering effort.

list.item: WOM: user achieves their goal; user discovers unexpected value; user solves a major problem
Inherent: user needs collaboration; user wants to share work; user requires team input
Incentivized: user completes onboarding; user reaches usage milestone; user is on referral page
Content: user creates something; user achieves a result; user wants recognition/comparison

section.title: Required Instrumentation

rule.statement: Instrument invite_sent with loop_type, share_channel, and recipient_count properties before launch; without these, K cannot be diagnosed when it drops.
rule.rationale: K drops silently. A redesigned share button can drop i by 40% without any alert. Weekly K split by loop_type and share_channel is the minimum for actionable monitoring.

section.reference: Python simulator to compare K projections for different loop choices

antipattern.description: Agent recommends "make it inherent" for a non-collaborative product.
antipattern.reason: Forced inherent virality creates fake invite prompts that users reject. It kills NPS without raising K. Inherent virality only works when collaboration is the core value proposition.

antipattern.description: Computing K on fewer than 500 invite events and reporting it as meaningful.
antipattern.reason: K is volatile at small samples. Signal:noise ratio is unacceptable below ~500 invites per reporting period. Agent must flag low-power computations explicitly.

antipattern.description: Recommending outbreak/spam loops based on outdated case studies.
antipattern.reason: Pre-2005 outbreak mechanics are blocked by modern email filters and mobile OS permissions. Always reject this suggestion regardless of source.
